# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure VMs with public IP addresses for direct access. Assign public IPs to each VM so developers can RDP/SSH directly without setting up a Bastion host or VPN. This simplifies our development environment setup.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
  - name: vm1-public-ip
    type: Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses
    apiVersion: 2019-06-01
    location: West US
    properties:
      publicIPAllocationMethod: Static
      dnsSettings:
        domainNameLabel: vm1

  - name: vm2-public-ip
    type: Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses
    apiVersion: 2019-06-01
    location: West US
    properties:
      publicIPAllocationMethod: Static
      dnsSettings:
        domainNameLabel: vm2

resources:
  - name: vm1
    type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines
    apiVersion: 2018-04-02
    location: West US
    dependsOn:
      - vm1-public-ip
    properties:
      hardwareProfile:
        vmSize: Standard_DS2_v2
      osProfile:
        computerName: vm1
        adminUsername: adminuser
        adminPassword: password
      storageProfile:
        imageReference:
          publisher: MicrosoftWindowsServer
          offer: WindowsServer
          sku: 2019-Datacenter
          version: latest
      networkProfile:
        networkInterfaces:
          - id: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/defaultGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces/vm1-nic

resources:
  - name: vm2
    type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines
    apiVersion: 2018-04-02
    location: West US
    dependsOn:
      - vm2-public-ip
    properties:
      hardwareProfile:
        vmSize: Standard_DS2_v2
      osProfile:
        computerName: vm2
        adminUsername: adminuser
        adminPassword: password
      storageProfile:
        imageReference:
          publisher: MicrosoftWindowsServer
          offer: WindowsServer
          sku: 2019-Datacenter
          version: latest
      networkProfile:
        networkInterfaces:
          - id: /subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/defaultGroup/providers/Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces/vm2-nic

outputs:
  vm1-ip: { value: 'vm1-public-ip.ipAddress' }
  vm2-ip: { value: 'vm2-public-ip.ipAddress' }
